stat_of_week: 8 - Sugar Land Skeeters posted a 2.11 ERA across 64.0 innings this week, the lowest ERA by any team in a winning effort this season, narrowly edging the previous record of 2.10 set by General Hospital in Week 2
thriller_note: The only tie of the week came down to a perfect split across nine categories, with Buttville's elite ERA (2.25) and pitching dominance neutralized by General Hospital's RBI edge (30 vs 22) and strikeout volume (70 vs 39), leaving both teams without a clean win and stalling momentum entering a pivotal Week 4.
key_storyline: Sugar Land Skeeters continued their dominance with an 8–2 demolition of the Abbotsford-Colby Twins, their second consecutive week leading the league in category wins and their third straight dominant performance to open the season. Matt Olson (3 HR, 8 RBI, .375 OBP), Drake Baldwin (.406 OBP), and a pitching staff that posted a jaw-dropping 2.11 ERA over 64 innings made them look like the clear class of the league. Meanwhile, Hiroshima Radiation exploded for an 8–2 win of their own over Canberra Wombats, reversing two weeks of underperformance and signaling that their roster may finally be firing on all cylinders.
lucky_reason: The Benchwarmers posted the highest luck delta in the league (+0.91) this week, stealing a 6–3 win over the Riverside Roughnecks despite a bloated 5.53 ERA and a league-worst 42.1 innings pitched from their rotation, winning primarily on the backs of their hitters while their pitching staff was a liability.
unlucky_reason: Despite outpacing the Albuquerque Bombers in strikeouts (62 vs 39), ERA (4.30 vs 5.05), WHIP (1.13 vs 1.39), QS (5 vs 3), and NSVH (7 vs 1), the Wonderdogs fell 5–4 and carry the season's worst luck delta (-0.60), suggesting their strong pitching keeps going unrewarded in close category battles.
records_broken: North Correa's 11 stolen bases in Week 3 ties the current season record for most SB by a team in a single week, matching their own mark set previously — Oneil Cruz led the charge with 6 steals alone.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Three weeks of category dominance, elite pitching ERA of 2.11 this week, and an 8–2 beatdown make them the undisputed No. 1 | same
2. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood (3 HR, 8 RBI, .636 OBP) and Willson Contreras (3 HR, 11 RBI, .594 OBP) powered a 6–3 win; offense is elite | up
3. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker's massive 5-HR week carried a 5–3 win; consistent category producer across three weeks | same
4. Hiroshima Radiation | Bounced back emphatically with an 8–2 rout, 6 QS, and 75 Ks; Bryce Harper's .552 OBP is a weapon | up
5. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 6–3 but posted a freakish 1.65 ERA and 1.04 WHIP; pitching is elite, offense needs to wake up | down
6. General Hospital | Tied despite Manny Machado (3 HR, 11 RBI) and a 70-K pitching week; 5.48 ERA is a concern but talent is undeniable | same
7. North Correa | 11 SB week is elite and Chris Sale delivered 22 Ks, but 4.73 ERA and only 51.1 IP hurt badly in the loss | same
8. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6–3 on the back of Trevor Story's 9 RBI despite zero QS and a 5.04 ERA; fluky but effective | up
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Acuña, Turang, and Stewart all had strong weeks, but the pitching (5.05 ERA) cost them a winnable matchup | same
10. Buttville Skidmarks | Elly De La Cruz is electric and the ERA (2.25) is elite, but a tie against General Hospital with this pitching staff is a missed opportunity | down
11. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Crushed 8–2 despite decent pitching (3.99 ERA, 75 Ks); bats outside Nimmo and Alvarez are underperforming | down
12. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Shota Imanaga (2.70 ERA, 14 Ks) and a 4.30 team ERA should be winning more; the luck just isn't coming | same
13. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson (5 HR, 8 RBI) is a stud but the team fell 8–2; pitching gave up too many categories | down
14. Benchwarmers | Won this week but carried by hitters alone; 42.1 IP and a 5.53 ERA is unsustainable and the luck will run out | same
